title: Orbital Selective Dirac-like States in EuAgAs Revealed by Polarization Dependent ARPES and DFT
authors: Mohit Mudgal, Suman Nandi, Mohamed El Gazzah, Masashi Arita, Shin-ichiro Ideta, Nirmal J. Ghimire, Kenya Shimada, Anup Pradhan Sakhya
abstract: Magnetic topological semimetals provide a promising platform for emergent quantum phenomena driven by the interplay between magnetism and relativistic fermions, including anomalous transport effects and tunable topological phases. Here, we investigate the electronic structure and orbital character of EuAgAs, a magnetic topological Dirac semimetal candidate, using density functional theory (DFT) and polarization dependent angle resolved photoemission spectroscopy (ARPES). Fermi surface mapping and constant energy contours measured at 9 eV reveal ring like features that systematically expand with increasing binding energy, consistent with nearly linear low energy Dirac like dispersion. ARPES measurements at different photon energies hint at the presence of a van Hove singularity predicted by DFT calculations. Furthermore, this indicates that the photoemission matrix elements are highly sensitive to the excitation energy, allowing different photon energies to selectively probe distinct orbital characters. Polarization dependent ARPES measurements performed in s- and p-polarized geometries exhibit pronounced variations in spectral intensity, indicating symmetry selective orbital contributions to electronic states. These matrix element driven intensity modulations are well reproduced by DFT calculations. Furthermore, the observed Dirac like states remain nearly unchanged over the temperature range from 9 K to 30 K, suggesting that the magnetic ordering has minimal influence on the electronic structure. Our combined experimental and theoretical results provide detailed insight into the orbital selective electronic structure of EuAgAs and its implications for magnetic topological quantum states.
summary: This paper uses polarization-dependent ARPES and DFT to reveal the orbital-selective electronic structure of the magnetic topological semimetal EuAgAs. It identifies Dirac-like states and a van Hove singularity that are robust against antiferromagnetic ordering. The findings provide crucial insight into how orbital symmetry and magnetism interplay in topological materials.
main_problem: The study aims to investigate the electronic structure, orbital character, and the impact of antiferromagnetic ordering on the low-energy states of the magnetic topological semimetal candidate EuAgAs.
main_result: The researchers identified orbital-selective Dirac-like states and a higher-order van Hove singularity, finding that the low-energy electronic structure remains largely unaffected by the antiferromagnetic transition at 12 K.
method: The study combines polarization-dependent and photon-energy-dependent ARPES with Density Functional Theory (DFT) calculations using the VASP package and Wannier90.
model_or_system: The physical system is EuAgAs, a magnetic topological Dirac semimetal candidate with a hexagonal ZrBeSi-type structure and an antiferromagnetic ground state.
key_observables: Fermi surface mapping, constant energy contours, band dispersions, spectral intensity variations under different polarizations, and magnetic susceptibility.
important_parameters: Néel temperature (TN) approx 12 K, photon energies ranging from 9 to 32 eV, and temperature range from 9 K to 30 K.
main_assumptions: The study assumes the validity of the DFT+U formalism for Eu 4f states and uses the GGA-PBE functional with spin-orbit coupling.
figures_summary: Fig 1 shows magnetic structure and calculated bands; Fig 2 compares experimental and theoretical Fermi surfaces; Fig 3 illustrates 3D band dispersions and van Hove singularities; Fig 4 shows temperature-dependent FS maps; Fig 5 demonstrates orbital-selective intensity modulation via polarization.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the material and its magnetic properties, presents experimental ARPES results (Fermi surface and dispersion), compares these with DFT calculations to identify orbital character, investigates the 3D nature of the bands, and discusses the stability of the electronic states across the magnetic transition.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. (a) Magnetic ground state structure of EuAgAs [29]. (b) Calculated electronic band structures of EuAgAs, including SOC for the AFM and NM phases, are shown in blue and red, respectively. The dashed line represents the shifted Fermi level. The right panel presents a zoomed in picture of (b) along the Γ- A direction, showing the DP near the Fermi level in the NM phase. (c) Schematic representation of the bulk and surface BZs with the corresponding high symmetry points indicated. (d) Temperature dependent magnetic susceptibility of EuAgAs measured under ZFC and FC conditions with a magnetic field (H = 0.1 T) applied parallel to the ab plane. (e) Temperature dependent heat capacity of...

FIG. 2. (Left panel) ARPES-measured Fermi surface and constant energy CECs at different binding energies acquired using a photon energy of 9 eV. The high symmetry directions are indicated. (Right panel) Corresponding DFT calculated surface projected Fermi surface and CECs showing the evo- lution of the electronic states with binding energy. The mea- surements were performed at the HiSOR BL-9A beamline at 9 K using p-polarized light.

FIG. 3. (a) ARPES-measured three dimensional band dispersions along the high symmetry directions M - Γ - M and K - Γ - K acquired at photon energies of 9 eV, 21 eV, and 32 eV. The corresponding Fermi surfaces measured at each photon energy are shown at the top of the three dimensional band dispersions. (b) Theoretically calculated electronic band dispersions along the K - Γ - M direction at different kz values. The right most panel represents the surface projected band structure. (c) Theoretically calculated band dispersions in kx-kz and ky-kz planes, revealing the vHS at Γ.

FIG. 4. Temperature dependent CECs and electronic band dispersion of EuAgAs along the high symmetry direction K - Γ - K. ARPES measured CECs at E −EF = 0 eV acquired using a photon energy of 9 eV at temperatures of (a) 9 K and (b) 30 K. Experimentally measured band dispersions along the K - Γ - K direction at (c) 9 K and (d) 30 K. MDCs along the K - Γ - K direction at (e) E −EF = 0 eV and (f) E −EF = −0.1 eV, comparing the temperature evolution of the electronic structure across the AFM transition. All measurements were performed at the HiSOR BL-9A beamline using p-polarized light.

Fig. 3(b) presents the theoretically calculated band dis- persions along the K -Γ- M direction at different kz values to understand the experimentally observed kz-dependent evolution. The right most panel of Fig. 3(b), showing the surface projected spectral function, qualitatively repro- duces the experimentally observed bands. With increas- ing kz, the upper portion of the hole like band gradually shifts toward higher energy, as indicated by the arrow in Fig. 3(b). This evolution signifies that the curvature of the band along the kz direction is opposite to that along the in plane kx and ky directions.
